zh.lifeSummary: 當Mabel Margaret Middleton於1485年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德米德萊頓時，她的父親Sir Geoffrey Middleton5歲，母親Lady Margaret Kirkham1歲。她於1515年與Roland Thomas Thornborough在英國維斯特莫藍德米德萊頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1570年1月21日在英國維斯特莫藍德享年85歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mabel Margaret Middleton于1485年出生在英国維斯特莫藍德米德萊頓时，她的父亲Sir Geoffrey Middleton5岁，母亲Lady Margaret Kirkham1岁。她于1515年与Roland Thomas Thornborough在英国維斯特莫藍德米德萊頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1570年1月21日在英国維斯特莫藍德享年85岁。

nameMeanings.name: Middleton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so called. In over thirty instances from many different areas, the name is from Old English midel ‘middle’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. However, Middleton on the Hill near Leominster in Herefordshire appears in Domesday Book as Miceltune, the first element clearly being Old English micel ‘large’, ‘great’ (see Middlebrook ). Middleton Baggot and Middleton Priors in Shropshire have early spellings that suggest gem̄thhyll (from gem̄th ‘confluence’ + hyll ‘hill’) + tūn as the origin. This surname is also found in Scotland and Ireland.

History: A Scottish family of this name derives it from lands at Middleto(u)n near Kincardine. The Scottish physician Peter Middleton practiced in New York City after 1752 and was one of the founders of the medical school at King's College (now Columbia University) in 1767. One of the earliest of the Charleston, SC, Middleton family of prominent legislators was Arthur Middleton, born in Charleston in 1681. The name has also been established in Ireland since at least the 13th century; its presence was reinforced by settlers in the 17th century.

variants.name: Midthun
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of about ten farmsteads in western Norway named Midtun, from Old Norse mith ‘middle’ + tún ‘enclosure, farmstead’. Compare Middleton .

variants.name: Melton
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Yorkshire, all of which have the same origin as Middleton , with Old English middel replaced by its Old Norse equivalent methal after the Scandinavian settlement of northern and eastern England.

variants.name: Middlebrook
meanings: English: habitational name from a lost Micklebrook in Blackburn (Lancashire), recorded as Mikelbroc in the 13th century. The placename appears to derive from Old English micel ‘big’ + brōc ‘brook, stream’. The surname has undergone a dissimilatory or folk-etymological change from Mickle- to Middle-.

variants.name: Milton
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the many places called Milton, or sometimes Middleton (see Middleton ) and occasionally Melton (see Melton ), most of which derive from Old English middel ‘middle’ or myln ‘mill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Mabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname from the Old French vocabulary word amabel, amable ‘lovely’ (akin to modern English amiable ‘friendly, good-humoured’). The initial vowel began to be lost as early as the 12th century (the same woman is referred to as both Mabilia and Amabilia in a document of 1185 ), but a short vowel in the resulting first syllable was standard, giving a rhyme with babble, until the 19th century, when people began to pronounce the name to rhyme with table.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Mabel Margaret Middleton was born in 1485, in Middleton, Westmorland, England, her father, Sir Geoffrey Middleton, was 5 and her mother, Lady Margaret Kirkham, was 1. She married Roland Thomas Thornborough in 1515, in Middleton, Westmorland, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 21 January 1570, in Westmorland, England, at the age of 85.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mabel Margaret Middleton est née en 1485, à Middleton, Westmorland, Angleterre, son père, Sir Geoffrey Middleton, avait 5 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Kirkham, avait 1 ans. Elle a épousé Roland Thomas Thornborough en 1515, à Middleton, Westmorland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 janvier 1570, à Westmorland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mabel Margaret Middleton 1485 in Middleton, Westmorland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Geoffrey Middleton 5 und ihre Mutter Lady Margaret Kirkham 1. Sie hat 1515 in Middleton, Westmorland, England Roland Thomas Thornborough geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 21. Januar 1570 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Westmorland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mabel Margaret Middleton è nata il nel 1485 nel Middleton, Westmorland, England, sua padre, Sir Geoffrey Middleton, aveva 5 anni e sua madre, Lady Margaret Kirkham, aveva 1 anni. Lei sposato Roland Thomas Thornborough nel 1515, nel Middleton, Westmorland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 21 gennaio 1570, nel Westmorland, England, all'età di 85 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mabel Margaret Middletonが1485年にイギリスウエストモーランドミッドレトンで生まれた時，父Sir Geoffrey Middletonは5歳，母Lady Margaret Kirkhamは1歳であった。1515年にイギリスウエストモーランドミッドレトンでRoland Thomas Thornboroughと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1570年1月21日にイギリスウエストモーランドで85歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mabel Margaret Middleton(이)가 1485년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 미들레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Geoffrey Middleton(은)는 5세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret Kirkham(은)는 1세였다. 그녀는 1515년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 미들레턴에서 Roland Thomas Thornborough(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1570년 1월 21일 영국 웨스트모를랜드에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mabel Margaret Middleton nasceu em 1485, em Middleton, Westmorland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Geoffrey Middleton, tinha 5 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret Kirkham, tinha 1 anos. Ela casou-se com Roland Thomas Thornborough em 1515, em Middleton, Westmorland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 21 de janeiro de 1570, em Westmorland, Inglaterra, com 85 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mabel Margaret Middleton родилась в 1485 г. в местности миддлетон, вестморланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Geoffrey Middleton было 5 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Kirkham, 1. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roland Thomas Thornborough в 1515 г. в местности миддлетон, вестморланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 21 января 1570 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия в возрасте 85 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mabel Margaret Middleton nació en 1485, en Middleton, Westmorland, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Geoffrey Middleton, tenía 5 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret Kirkham, tenía 1 años. Se casó con Roland Thomas Thornborough en 1515, en Middleton, Westmorland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 21 de enero de 1570, en Westmorland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 85 años.
